name: Tutu a Mineira ( Bean Puree)
recipeIngredient: water to cover beans by 6 inches
1/2 to 1 cup corn flour or yucca flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the black beans overnight or place them in a stockpot with cold water and bring to a boil. Cover and turn off heat, letting stand for 1 hour.
2. Add bacon, 1 sausage, and bay leaf; bring to a boil. Turn down the heat and simmer covered until beans are cooked.
3. Heat oil in a pan and cook chopped onion and garlic until onions are translucent.
4. Place beans, cooked onion mixture, salt, pepper, and parsley into a food processor (you may need to do this in parts) and run the machine until it becomes creamy.
5. Return the cream to the pot and bring to a boil while stirring.
6. Gradually add corn flour to the beans, stirring well as if making polenta.
7. Keep stirring until thick like a puree.
8. Meanwhile, bake kielbasa to taste.
9. Fry sliced onions in oil until soft.
10. Serve with baked kielbasa, fried onions decorated with quartered hard-boiled eggs. Serve with kale, rice, and very hot chili on the side.
